findings_identified.description: dignity-instrument session had two same-agent outbound messages to observatory at turn 4: to-observatory-agent-001.json (draft relay, macOS gray-box instance) and to-observatory-agent-003.json (revised relay, Chromebook instance). Both were delivered before machines were coordinated.
findings_identified.root_cause: Two agent instances (gray-box macOS and Chromebook) were both active during the dignity-instrument relay. The macOS instance sent the first relay; the Chromebook instance sent a revised version. No session-level deduplication existed at the time.
findings_identified.remediation_applied: Updated MANIFEST.json to annotate to-observatory-agent-001.json with supersession note identifying it as draft superseded by to-observatory-agent-003.json. Observatory's T4 ACK references the relay generically — session outcome unaffected.

findings_identified.description: local-coordination session MANIFEST had 139 messages all at turn=0. MANIFEST turn structure check flagged this as same-agent duplicate turns.
findings_identified.root_cause: local-coordination is an operational log session for daemon heartbeat, escalation, and halt records — not an interagent/v1 protocol session. Files use timestamps in filenames (e.g., halt-unratified-agent-20260311T040102.json), not sequential NNN. Turn=0 is a MANIFEST generation artifact, not a protocol defect.
findings_identified.remediation_applied: Added session_type: 'local-coordination' and turn_note to MANIFEST.json explaining turn=0 is by design for this non-protocol operational log. No protocol messages are affected.

non_findings_noted: psq-scoring: from-observatory-agent-psq-t16.json vs from-observatory-agent-001.json at turn 16 — observatory-agent naming inconsistency on sender side. Not a unratified-agent transport defect.
from-observatory-agent-003.json and from-observatory-agent-004.json both at dignity-instrument T7 — observatory duplicate ACK delivery. Annotated in MANIFEST. Not our defect.
Multi-recipient same-turn patterns (mesh-init, dignity-instrument) — by-design for multi-party sessions: one agent sends to N recipients at same turn. Not a duplicate turn defect.
